steps.title: Recognize what the variables represent in m(t)
steps.content: The function $m(t)$ is defined with $t$ representing days after birth (input) and $m(t)$ representing body mass in kg (output). This assignment of variables is crucial for interpreting the given statement.

steps.title: Interpret m(330) ≈ 362 using the variable definitions
steps.content: Given that $m(330) \approx 362$, we identify: $t = 330$ (days after birth) is the input, and $m(330) \approx 362$ kg (body mass) is the output. Therefore, 330 days after it was born, the animal's predicted body mass was approximately 362 kg. That matches choice B — B is correct.
